archimedes-screw-requires-water: Requires vanilla or Archimedes water at the intake position
archimedes-screw-endcap-context: Place the end cap in vanilla or Archimedes water (intake) or on top of a straight screw or intake (outlet)
config-max-controllers-per-global-tick: Max intakes processed per dispatcher tick
config-max-blocks-per-step: Sources removed per fast tick when draining
config-max-vanilla-conversion-passes: Vanilla source conversion passes per tick
config-required-mech-power-for-max-relay: Required mechanical power for max relays
handbook-guide-text:
Archimedes Screw
A powered pump for lifting and routing water.
The Archimedes Screw is a vertical multiblock that draws water at the intake and maintains water at the outlet. It creates a single source block at the outlet, which is maintained as long as mechanical power to the screw is maintained. If the screw loses power, or the flow is interrupted for any other reason, the created water is removed, along with any water that has been placed inside the water stream.

Assembly Basics
Build from bottom to top: place an end-cap in water to create an intake, stack straight screw segments above it, and place an end-cap on top to create an outlet. A valid assembly requires one intake at the bottom, one outlet at the top, and only straight segments between them. You can right-click on any of the archimedes screw parts to view the current status of the assembly. Mechanical power can be fed in through the top or bottom of the assembly.

Server tuning
With the Config Lib mod installed, edit settings in-game or in ModConfig/archimedes_screw.yaml; those values patch config/settings.json. Saving in Config Lib applies tuning on the running server without a restart. Without Config Lib, defaults come from the mod’s packaged config/settings.json only (edit the mod files to change them).
